# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Istio IngressGateway configuration that routes HTTP traffic to services. Configure Gateway for port 80 without TLS for simplicity. Use HTTP for all ingress traffic since we have a load balancer handling TLS termination upstream. This is for our Istio service mesh ingress.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent service mesh security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: istio-ingressgateway   # Name of the gateway resource in Istio namespace (default to "<namespace>" if not specified)
spec:
  selector:
    istio: ingressgateway     # This label selects this as our Ingress Gateways's destination rule. Required by Envoy sidecar proxies for routing traffic between services in the mesh.  
  servers:                    # List of gateways server definitions (one per port)   
  - port:                      # Port number on which to expose service, can be a numerical or named port i.e., http2/http3 ports and others like grpc etc.. Can also use names if specified in the Gateway's metadata section  
      name:                     # Name of this server for reference purposes (optional) 
        hostname:                # Hostnames to match on, can be a comma separated list or an IP address. Required by Envoy sidecar proxies and Istio routing rules   
          - "*"                  # Listening hosts in the gateway's server block are specified as wildcards (*) which means all incoming requests will go to this port  
  route:                      # Route configuration for outbound traffic (one or more routes per Gateway, can be a subset of DestinationRules/VirtualServices defined elsewhere). Required by Envoy sidecar proxies and Istio routing rules   
  - destination:              # Name to select the virtual service that will handle requests from this gateway. This is required for traffic flow in mesh  
      host: "*"                 # Hostname or IP address of a Service being requested, can be specified as wildcards (*) which means all services within Istio's control plane are accessible through Gateway
